### Post by overdrank on 2007-09-24
HI what video card? and you may add this to the xorg at the end
Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite Enable"
EndSection

### Post by mysticmatrix on 2007-09-25
> **phizikal said:**
> I am getting the errors when I type in beryl in the shell, or try run any games that require 3D.

example...

Beryl is known not to run on your card, minimum requirement is Geforce 2 I think.
Don't know about the games though, but more or less I think you would need a upgrade.

And was there any reason of using ENVY? Restricted manager works just fine on one of my friends old PC.

From Beryl's Website

 > What are the system requirements for Beryl?
    Beryl runs acceptably well on a GeForce 3/i855/Radeon 7500, 256MB of RAM, and a 1.2GHz processor. It also works best with Xorg 7.1 and requires a recent version of Mesa.

### Post by Ant_Merlin on 2007-09-25
Hi, Xlib extension GLX missing on display ":0.0", you are running GLX right? well 3D games dont work with GLX sessions. THis is due to GLX creating an extra Display (DISPLAY 1) for its 3d desktop effects. You need to either run games from normal gnome session or I have found this to work:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=557909]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=557909")
Let me know how you get on.

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2007-09-25
When you loaded your video drivers using ENVY did you first off remove the old drivers.  Then did you install the new drivers.  I found I got the same error when I did not remove the old drivers which were "nv".

---

### Post by phizikal on 2007-09-25
Ok, how am i am gonna uninstall the old drivers?

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2007-09-25
You have the option to install and remove drivers in Envy.
